summary: Track the remaining readiness blocker after switching auto-improve to live-by-default, verifying serial gates, seeding one narrow finalized fallback run, adding an explicit readiness preflight/helper target, rechecking queue truth from comparable run history, and selecting the next blocker-reduction maintenance task.

observations.problem: The original blocker was empty history for `ops/scripts/auto_improve_iteration_persistence_runtime.py`, but after the comparable run `run-20260422-auto-improve-decision-record-fallback-retry3-linux-tmp` the review queue changed shape: mechanism review now emits one stagnation candidate and mutation proposal emits one proposal, yet that proposal is blocked by `recent_log_overlap`. The first readiness helper version treated `summary.proposals_emitted > 0` as queue-ready even though `auto_improve_queue_runtime.build_proposal_queue()` would return no runnable proposals.
observations.why_it_matters: At this point the repo is no longer blocked by missing comparable history, but it is still not safe to launch live auto-improve. Operators need readiness to reflect the same runnable-queue truth the loop uses, otherwise the preflight can incorrectly recommend starting a session that will immediately exhaust the queue.
observations.automation_candidate: Keep using `make auto-improve-readiness`, but make it derive readiness from runnable proposals after `blocked_by` filtering instead of raw `proposals_emitted`, and surface the remaining blocker when chronology overlap keeps every emitted proposal paused.
observations.suggested_followup: Updated on 2026-04-22 KST: the threshold review still supports keeping current mechanism-review thresholds, and the comparable narrow run requirement is now satisfied enough to emit `mechanism_eval_stagnation_candidate__auto-improve-iteration-persistence-runtime`. Automated on 2026-04-23 KST so readiness now derives queue readiness from runnable proposals after `blocked_by` filtering and exposes `queue.runnable_proposal_ids`, `queue.blocked_reason_counts`, and `fallback.status=blocked_queue` when proposals exist but all are blocked. After a current non-overlapping closeout log advances the timestamp-sorted recent-log window, `make auto-improve-readiness` should require `runnable_proposal_count >= 1` before live auto-improve is considered ready.

observations.problem: The current generated queue has one emitted proposal and zero runnable proposals because `recent_log_overlap` is stored as a hard `blocked_by` reason; this is correct for the overlapping target, but blocked proposals can still shape readiness and proposal-slot pressure more strongly than their runnable status warrants.
observations.why_it_matters: The next maintenance step should reduce queue blocker friction without clearing the chronology guard or re-running the same recently discarded target, so auto-improve stays paused while the queue policy becomes better at surfacing runnable alternatives when they exist.
observations.automation_candidate: Refine mutation proposal and readiness queue policy so blocked proposals remain visible with reason counts but do not crowd out runnable alternatives or make raw emitted proposal counts look queue-ready.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-04-22 KST by making mutation proposal report selection prefer unblocked proposals before blocked ones, adding `diagnostics.queue_selection.blocked_reason_counts`, and covering blocked-slot plus mixed runnable/blocked readiness behavior. Updated on 2026-04-23 KST by adding structured readiness `remediations` so `recent_log_overlap` remains a hard block while the report also exposes remediation_code, blocker_kind, unblock_action_type, minimum_evidence, and retry_condition. Updated again on 2026-04-23 KST by expanding mutation proposals with `blast_radius_score`, `must_change_tests`, `must_change_budget_signal`, `must_not_expand_apply_roots`, and `must_not_increase_untyped_surface`, plus a lower-blast-radius tie-break during report top-N selection. Updated once more on 2026-04-23 KST so `must_change_tests` can fall back to content-linked existing tests when scope glob resolution misses a narrow runtime target such as `ops/scripts/auto_improve_iteration_persistence_runtime.py`. Completed the current queue-policy follow-up on 2026-04-23 KST by sorting recent-log sections by parsed timestamp, retaining `recent_log_overlap` as a hard blocker, and recording matched marker, matched log heading, dedupe window, and unblock condition in mutation-proposal diagnostics. Keep the chronology guard hard; fresh runnable proposals should appear only when the timestamp-sorted recent-log window no longer overlaps the target.
